title: Responsive layout fix + complete professional admin panel

requirements.summary: Fix responsive layout across the entire public-facing site for mobile (≤768px) and laptop (≥1024px) viewports
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal scroll on any public page at 375px viewport width
Hero image and headline are fully visible and readable on mobile
Quote form fields stack vertically and are full-width on mobile
Testimonial carousel, service cards, and footer all display correctly at both 375px and 1280px widths
All font sizes use responsive Tailwind classes (text-sm → text-xl scaling)

file_operations.description: Ensure the main container and all section wrappers use responsive Tailwind utilities (px-4 md:px-8, max-w-7xl mx-auto) to prevent horizontal overflow on mobile.

Make the hero section fully responsive: apply responsive text sizing (text-3xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl for headline), ensure agent portrait/hero image scales properly (max-w-full h-auto), and stack trust stats vertically on mobile using flex-col md:flex-row.
Ensure the header adapts correctly on mobile: logo and CTA buttons should remain visible and properly sized, and the mobile menu should open without layout issues.
Update service cards grid to stack on mobile (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3), ensure card text uses responsive font sizes, and add proper spacing (gap-4 md:gap-6).
Make testimonial carousel and agent bio responsive: stack content vertically on mobile, ensure carousel controls are touch-friendly and visible, and apply responsive text sizing.

Ensure the quote form fields stack vertically on mobile (w-full), use full-width inputs, apply responsive padding and spacing, and keep submit button accessible and sized appropriately for touch targets.
Update footer columns to stack vertically on mobile (flex-col md:flex-row or grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3), ensure social icons and contact info remain readable, and add proper vertical spacing.
Make the four value-prop cards stack on mobile (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4), ensure icons and text scale correctly, and apply responsive padding.
Update carrier badge grid to display 2 columns on mobile and 4 on desktop (grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4), ensure badges remain square and legible at all sizes.

requirements.summary: Add 'Agent Login' button in ReevesHeader that opens a modal with frontend credential check (admin / conroe123) and navigates to admin dashboard on success
acceptanceCriteria: 'Agent Login' button visible in header top-right on all screen sizes
Clicking button opens a centered modal overlay with username and password fields
Entering admin / conroe123 and submitting navigates to /admin/dashboard
Incorrect credentials display 'Invalid credentials' error inside the modal
Modal can be dismissed by clicking outside or an X button

file_operations.description: Add an 'Agent Login' button in the top-right corner next to the phone CTA. On click, open the AdminLoginModal. Ensure the button is visible and accessible on both mobile and desktop.

Create a modal component with username and password inputs. On form submit, check if credentials match admin / conroe123 (frontend check only). Store success state in sessionStorage as 'adminAuthenticated'. On success, close modal and use TanStack Router to navigate to /admin/dashboard. On failure, display 'Invalid credentials' error. Include an X button and outside-click handler to close the modal.

Create a custom hook that provides login, logout, and isAuthenticated functions. Login checks credentials and sets sessionStorage 'adminAuthenticated' to 'true'. Logout clears sessionStorage. isAuthenticated reads from sessionStorage and returns a boolean. This hook is used by AdminLoginModal and ProtectedAdminRoute.

requirements.summary: Build a complete admin dashboard page at /admin/dashboard with dark theme (#1a1a2e, #16213e, #0f3460), stat cards, leads table with filtering, Export CSV, and charts
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard page renders at /admin/dashboard with #1a1a2e/#16213e dark background
All 4 stat cards visible with correct values and trend indicators
Leads table shows at least 8 rows with Conroe TX names and 936-xxx-xxxx phone numbers
Filtering by New/Called/Won correctly shows only matching rows
Export CSV button downloads a CSV file containing the filtered leads data
Line chart renders leads per day for the past 7 days
Pie chart renders with correct percentage segments and legend labels

file_operations.description: Replace existing dashboard content with a complete dark-themed layout using #1a1a2e background. Render 4 stat cards (Total Leads: 127 +15%, Quotes: 23 18% conv, Calls: 45, Won: 8 $24K) at the top using responsive grid. Below, render the LeadsTable component, LeadsChart, and PolicyPieChart components. Apply dark theme colors (#16213e for cards, #0f3460 for accents) throughout.

Create a leads table component displaying columns: Name, Phone, Email, Policy, Status, Date. Pre-populate with at least 8 sample leads using Conroe TX first/last names (e.g., James Anderson, Maria Rodriguez) and 936-xxx-xxxx phone numbers. Include status filter tabs (New, Called, Won) that filter visible rows. Add an 'Export CSV' button that generates and downloads a CSV of the currently filtered data. Use dark theme colors and ensure table is horizontally scrollable on mobile.

Create a line chart component showing leads per day for the past 7 days using a charting library (e.g., recharts). Use mock data (e.g., [18, 22, 19, 25, 21, 23, 24] leads per day). Apply dark theme colors (#1a1a2e background, #0f3460 line color, white text). Ensure chart is responsive.

Create a pie chart component displaying policy type breakdown: Auto 45%, Home 30%, Life 15%, Commercial 10%. Use a charting library (e.g., recharts). Apply dark theme colors and include a legend. Ensure chart is responsive and readable on mobile.

Export an array of at least 8 mock lead objects with fields: name (Conroe TX names), phone (936-xxx-xxxx), email, policy (Auto/Home/Life/Commercial), status (New/Called/Won), date (recent dates). This data is imported and used by LeadsTable.

requirements.summary: Build admin panel side navigation and layout shell with Dashboard, Leads, Policy Management, Lead Assignment, Analytics, PDF Invoices sections and stub pages
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar navigation includes all 6 sections with active-state highlight using #0f3460
Policy Management page renders with a placeholder list
Lead Assignment page renders with a lead list and an agent assignment dropdown per row
Analytics page renders extended chart placeholders
PDF Invoices page lists invoices and each row has a 'Generate PDF' button that opens a printable invoice in a new tab or triggers window.print()

file_operations.description: Update AdminLayout to render a full-height dark sidebar (#1a1a2e) with navigation links: Dashboard, Leads, Policy Management, Lead Assignment, Analytics, PDF Invoices. Highlight active route with #0f3460 background. The main content area uses #16213e background. Include a header with admin branding and a logout button (using useAdminAuth hook). Render child routes via Outlet.
Update AdminLeadsPage to render the full LeadsTable component (created in REQ-3) with search, filtering, and export functionality. Ensure dark theme colors are applied.
Create a stub page displaying a placeholder message 'Policy Management - Coming Soon' and a mock table layout with columns: Policy ID, Client, Type, Status. Use dark theme colors.

Create a stub page displaying a list of leads (can reuse mockLeads data) with an 'Assign Agent' dropdown per row. Dropdowns show mock agent names (e.g., Agent A, Agent B). On selection, show a toast message confirming assignment (no backend call). Use dark theme colors.
Create a stub page with placeholder chart titles: 'Lead Conversion Funnel', 'Revenue by Policy Type', 'Monthly Trends'. Show empty chart containers or static placeholder text. Use dark theme colors.

Create a page displaying a table of mock invoices (Invoice ID, Client, Amount, Date). Each row has a 'Generate PDF' button. On click, open a new browser tab with a printable invoice template (simple HTML styled for print) or trigger window.print() on a hidden invoice element. Use dark theme colors.

Add new child routes under the /admin layout: /admin/policy-management, /admin/lead-assignment, /admin/analytics, /admin/pdf-invoices. Wire them to the corresponding page components. Ensure all routes are protected by the ProtectedAdminRoute guard.

requirements.summary: Make the admin panel fully responsive for mobile: hamburger menu, stacked stat cards, swipeable table
acceptanceCriteria: At 375px viewport, sidebar is hidden and a hamburger icon appears in the admin header
Tapping hamburger opens a full-height slide-out drawer with all nav links
Stat cards stack in a single column on mobile
Leads table is horizontally scrollable without clipping on mobile
All admin text and buttons remain readable and tappable on mobile

file_operations.description: Add responsive behavior: hide the sidebar on screens ≤768px using md:block hidden. Add a hamburger menu icon in the admin header (visible only on mobile). On click, toggle a slide-out drawer overlay that displays the same navigation links. Use Tailwind transition utilities for smooth open/close animation. Ensure drawer can be closed by clicking outside or a close button.
Ensure the 4 stat cards use a responsive grid: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4. This stacks cards vertically on mobile and displays them in a row on larger screens.

Wrap the table in a container with overflow-x-auto to enable horizontal scrolling on mobile. Ensure the table maintains a minimum width so all columns remain visible and swipeable. Add touch-friendly padding and ensure text remains legible at small sizes.

Create a reusable AdminHeader component that includes the hamburger menu icon (visible on mobile), admin branding, and a logout button. The hamburger icon toggles the mobile drawer state in AdminLayout. Use dark theme colors and ensure all elements are touch-friendly.

requirements.summary: Protect admin routes with authentication guard that redirects unauthenticated users and persists session in sessionStorage
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating directly to /admin/dashboard without being logged in redirects to the public home page
After successful login, refreshing the admin dashboard keeps the user logged in
Closing the browser tab and reopening clears the admin session
A logout button in the admin header clears session and redirects to home

file_operations.description: Update ProtectedAdminRoute to use the useAdminAuth hook. Check if isAuthenticated is true. If not, redirect to the public home page (/) using TanStack Router's Navigate component. If authenticated, render the Outlet to display child routes. Show a loading spinner while checking auth state.

Ensure the hook reads 'adminAuthenticated' from sessionStorage on mount and provides a logout function that clears sessionStorage and redirects to home. SessionStorage automatically clears when the browser tab is closed, meeting the requirement.
Add a logout button in the admin header that calls the logout function from useAdminAuth. On logout, clear sessionStorage and navigate to the public home page using TanStack Router.
Ensure all /admin/* routes are children of the ProtectedAdminRoute layout route so the authentication guard applies to all admin pages.
